# Dispersion Modeling

**Summary**: A mathematical model used to simulate how air pollutants disperse in the atmosphere from specific sources.

---

## How It Is Used in the Thesis Collection

- [[2025-al-afif|Al-Afif, 2025]] used CALPUFF dispersion modeling to estimate pollutant concentrations under different mitigation scenarios.

## Key Results and Findings

- The CALPUFF model successfully identified that fuel shifting resulted in a 97.2% reduction in SO2 emissions compared to the original case. ([[2025-al-afif|Al-Afif, 2025, p. 40]])
